subject: computer-science
description: Firsts in Computer Science MCQs for PPSC, FPSC, NTS, CSS & PMS.

mcqs.q: What was the first computer ever built?
mcqs.explanation: The first computer ever built was the Difference Engine in 1821 by Charles Babbage.

mcqs.q: In which year was the Difference Engine built?
mcqs.explanation: The Difference Engine — the first computer ever built, by Charles Babbage — was built in 1821.

mcqs.q: Who designed the Difference Engine?
mcqs.explanation: The Difference Engine was designed by Charles Babbage in 1821.

mcqs.q: What was the first general-purpose computer?
mcqs.explanation: The Analytical Engine (1834) by Charles Babbage was the first general-purpose computer.

mcqs.q: In which year was the Analytical Engine designed?
mcqs.explanation: The Analytical Engine — the first general-purpose computer — was designed by Charles Babbage in 1834.

mcqs.q: Who wrote the first computer program?
mcqs.explanation: The first computer program was written in 1841 by Ada Lovelace for Babbage's Analytical Engine.

mcqs.q: In which year was the first computer program written?
mcqs.explanation: The first computer program was written in 1841 by Ada Lovelace.

mcqs.q: Herman Hollerith is famous for inventing the:
mcqs.explanation: Herman Hollerith invented the Tabulating Machine in 1884 — the foundation of IBM's punched-card business.

mcqs.q: What was the first analog computer?
mcqs.explanation: The first analog computer was the Differential Analyzer, in 1930.

mcqs.q: What was the first working programmable computer?
mcqs.explanation: The first working programmable computer was the Z3, built in 1941.

mcqs.q: When was the first trackball developed?
mcqs.explanation: The first trackball was developed during a WWII radar project (1941/1952).

mcqs.q: What was the first electronic computer?
mcqs.explanation: The first electronic computer was the ABC (Atanasoff-Berry Computer), built in 1942.

mcqs.q: In which year was the ABC (Atanasoff-Berry Computer) built?
mcqs.explanation: The ABC (Atanasoff-Berry Computer) — the first electronic computer — was built in 1942.

mcqs.q: What was the first programmable computer?
mcqs.explanation: The first programmable computer was Colossus, built in 1943.

mcqs.q: In which year was Colossus built?
mcqs.explanation: Colossus — the first programmable computer — was built in 1943.

mcqs.q: What was the first general-purpose programmable electronic computer?
mcqs.explanation: ENIAC (1946) was the first general-purpose programmable electronic computer.

mcqs.q: In which year was ENIAC built?
mcqs.explanation: ENIAC — the first general-purpose programmable electronic computer — was built in 1946.

mcqs.q: What was the first complete high-level programming language?
mcqs.explanation: Plankalkül (1948) was the first complete high-level programming language.

mcqs.q: In which year was Plankalkül developed?
mcqs.explanation: Plankalkül — the first complete high-level language — was developed in 1948.

mcqs.q: What was the first stored-program electronic digital computer?
mcqs.explanation: The first stored-program electronic digital computer was the SSEM (Small-Scale Experimental Machine) in 1948.

mcqs.q: What was the first assembler?
mcqs.explanation: The first assembler was 'Initial Orders' in 1949.

mcqs.q: What was the first personal computer?
mcqs.explanation: The first personal computer was Simon, in 1950.

mcqs.q: In which year was the first personal computer (Simon) introduced?
mcqs.explanation: Simon — the first personal computer — was introduced in 1950.

mcqs.q: What was the first compiler for an electronic computer?
mcqs.explanation: The first compiler for an electronic computer was the A-0 System in 1951.

mcqs.q: In which year was the first compiler (A-0 System) developed?
mcqs.explanation: The first compiler — the A-0 System — was developed in 1951.

mcqs.q: What was the first real-time graphics display computer?
mcqs.explanation: The first real-time graphics display computer was the AN/FSQ-7 in 1951.

mcqs.q: What was the first autocode?
mcqs.explanation: Glennie's Autocode (1952) was the first autocode — an early form of high-level language.

mcqs.q: What was the first open-source software?
mcqs.explanation: The first open-source software was the A-2 System, in 1953.

mcqs.q: What was the first popular high-level programming language?
mcqs.explanation: FORTRAN (1957) was the first popular high-level programming language.

mcqs.q: In which year was FORTRAN released?
mcqs.explanation: FORTRAN — the first popular high-level language — was released in 1957.

mcqs.q: What was the first object-oriented programming language?
mcqs.explanation: Simula (1967) was the first object-oriented programming language.

mcqs.q: Who designed the Simula programming language?
mcqs.explanation: Simula was designed and built by Ole-Johan Dahl and Kristen Nygaard at the Norwegian Computing Center (NCC) in Oslo between 1962 and 1967.

mcqs.q: At which institution was Simula built?
mcqs.explanation: Simula was built at the Norwegian Computing Center (NCC) in Oslo, Norway between 1962 and 1967.

mcqs.q: Simula was designed for which primary purpose?
mcqs.explanation: Simula was designed for doing simulations — and the needs of that domain provided the framework for many of the features of modern object-oriented languages.

mcqs.q: Who invented the first computer mouse and in which year?
mcqs.explanation: The first computer mouse was invented by Douglas Engelbart in 1964.

mcqs.q: In which year was the first computer mouse invented?
mcqs.explanation: The first computer mouse was invented in 1964 by Douglas Engelbart.

mcqs.q: In which year was the first touchscreen introduced?
mcqs.explanation: The first touchscreen was introduced in 1965.

mcqs.q: What was the first commercial personal computer?
mcqs.explanation: The first commercial personal computer was the Programma 101, in 1965.

mcqs.q: What was the first microprocessor?
mcqs.explanation: The first microprocessor was the Intel 4004, in 1971.

mcqs.q: In which year was the Intel 4004 (first microprocessor) made commercially available?
mcqs.explanation: The Intel 4004 — the first microprocessor — was made commercially available in 1971.

mcqs.q: In which year was the Z3 — the first working programmable computer — built?
mcqs.explanation: The Z3 — the first working programmable computer — was built in 1941.

mcqs.q: In which year was the Differential Analyzer (first analog computer) introduced?
mcqs.explanation: The Differential Analyzer — the first analog computer — was introduced in 1930.

mcqs.q: In which year was the Tabulating Machine invented?
mcqs.explanation: Herman Hollerith's Tabulating Machine was invented in 1884.

mcqs.q: In which year was the first stored-program electronic digital computer (SSEM) operational?
mcqs.explanation: The SSEM (Small-Scale Experimental Machine) — the first stored-program electronic digital computer — was operational in 1948.

mcqs.q: Which of the following came FIRST historically?
mcqs.explanation: The Difference Engine (1821) came first historically — it predates ENIAC, FORTRAN and the Intel 4004.

mcqs.q: Which is the correct chronological order?
options: Difference Engine → Analytical Engine → ENIAC → Intel 4004
ENIAC → Intel 4004 → Analytical Engine → Difference Engine
Intel 4004 → Difference Engine → ENIAC → Analytical Engine
mcqs.explanation: Correct chronological order: Difference Engine (1821) → Analytical Engine (1834) → ENIAC (1946) → Intel 4004 (1971).

mcqs.q: In which year was the first commercial personal computer (Programma 101) released?
mcqs.explanation: The Programma 101 — the first commercial personal computer — was released in 1965.

mcqs.q: Which is older: Plankalkül (first complete high-level language) or FORTRAN (first popular high-level language)?
mcqs.explanation: Plankalkül (1948) is older than FORTRAN (1957) — Plankalkül was the first complete high-level language but never widely used; FORTRAN was the first popular one.

mcqs.q: What was the first open-source software (A-2 System) released in?
mcqs.explanation: The A-2 System — the first open-source software — was released in 1953.

mcqs.q: Which year saw both the first touchscreen AND the first commercial personal computer?
mcqs.explanation: 1965 saw both the first touchscreen AND the first commercial personal computer (Programma 101).
